lib.title: Detectives
lib.arch: Sapphire/Steel challenge midrange — ramp into durable Detectives, use Shift characters and Resist to dominate combat, then turn every successful challenge into cards and bonus lore.
lib.tagline: Solve the case, win the fight, collect the lore.
lib.strategy:
Core strategy

This deck does not want to race by questing immediately. It wants to build a board of Detectives, make extremely efficient challenges, and profit every time an opposing character is banished.

Your ideal development is:

Nick Wilde → Darkwing Duck or Judy → ramp/value item → Shift payoff → repeated challenge turns.

Early game

Your best opening characters are:

Nick Wilde – Sly Fox Sleuth: cheap Detective and Shift target.
Darkwing Duck – Drake Mallard: a 2-cost, 4-Strength Darkwing with Ward. He pressures fragile characters and enables both large Darkwing Shift options.
Judy Hopps – Snowball Patrol: especially strong when playing second because she can cost 1 on your first turn.
Darkwing’s Chair Set: ramps one card from the top of your deck into the inkwell, accelerating your expensive characters. It can later be banished to remove up to 4 damage from a Darkwing.

Do not challenge recklessly with your small Nick or Judy when they are your only Shift targets. Preserving one base character can be more valuable than making a minor trade.

Main value engine
Nick Wilde – Persistent Investigator

Shift Nick for 3 whenever possible. During your turn, each time one of your Detectives banishes an opposing character in a challenge, Nick draws a card. This turns combat from a one-for-one exchange into sustained card advantage.

Judy Hopps – Lead Detective

Judy is the deck’s main combat commander. During your turn, all your Detectives gain:

Alert, allowing them to challenge Evasive characters.
Resist +2, making your challenges much safer.

With Mrs. Incredible – Determined Rescuer, your characters gain another Resist +1. A Detective can therefore have Resist +3 during your challenge turn, allowing it to survive fights that would normally trade evenly.

Ranger Plane

Ranger Plane gives all your characters Support. You can quest with a smaller character and transfer its Strength to the Detective that needs to make the important challenge. This allows your board to generate normal lore while preparing a stronger attack.

Challenge-to-lore engine

The deck’s most important feature is that challenging becomes another way to win the lore race.

The Sword of Hercules: each time one of your characters banishes another character in a challenge during your turn, gain 1 lore.
The Thunderquack: after a character has been banished in a challenge, each Thunderquack can exert for 1 lore.
Darkwing Duck & Launchpad – St. Canard’s Finest: gains 2 lore whenever it banishes another character in a challenge.
Nick Wilde – Persistent Investigator: draws a card when one of your Detectives makes that banish.
Absorbing Bloom: can draw a card after a challenge banish.

A single successful challenge can therefore produce:

Board removal + card draw + 2 or more bonus lore.

With several payoff items in play, you can win without exposing your entire board by questing.

Darkwing combo

The Thunderquack gives every opposing character the Villain classification. Darkwing Duck – Cool Under Pressure can challenge ready Villains, so the opponent cannot protect key characters simply by leaving them ready.

Cool Under Pressure also triggers whenever an item is banished. You may pay 1 ink to deal 2 damage to a chosen character. The cleanest interaction is:

Banish Darkwing’s Chair Set.
Remove up to 4 damage from a Darkwing.
Pay 1 with Cool Under Pressure to deal 2 damage.
Darkwarrior gains Resist +1 from the banished item.
Finish the damaged opposing character in a challenge.

This creates removal, healing and combat protection from one item.

Removal package

Use your removal to prepare profitable challenges rather than spending it automatically:

The Terror That Flaps in the Night: deals 2 damage, or 3 while you control a Darkwing.
He Hurled His Thunderbolt: deals 4 damage.
Let It Go: answers large characters that are too difficult or dangerous to challenge.
Judy Hopps – On the Case: can place an opposing item into its owner’s inkwell when you already control another Detective.

Damage actions are often best used to soften a character so one of your Detectives can finish it. That final challenge activates Nick, Thunderquack, Sword, Bloom and St. Canard’s Finest.

Primary win conditions
1. Repeated Detective challenges

Establish Nick and Judy, then continuously banish characters while drawing replacements and protecting your board with Resist.

2. Darkwing & Launchpad challenge turns

Shift it for 5 onto an inexpensive Darkwing. It has Ward, 5 Strength, 7 Willpower and earns 2 lore for each character it banishes in a challenge. Support and Judy’s Resist make it difficult to stop in combat.

3. Traditional questing

Against decks that offer few challenge targets, use your durable characters to quest normally. Judy Uncovering Clues helps maintain your hand by finding another Detective when played and whenever she quests.

Mulligan
Prioritize
Nick Wilde – Sly Fox Sleuth
Darkwing Duck – Drake Mallard
Judy Hopps – Snowball Patrol when playing second
Darkwing’s Chair Set with an early character
Nick Wilde – Persistent Investigator alongside Nick
Judy Hopps – Lead Detective alongside a cheaper Judy
One Darkwing Shift payoff alongside Drake Mallard
Usually return
Multiple copies of Let It Go
Mrs. Incredible without sufficient early plays
Standalone 7-cost characters without Shift targets
The Sword of Hercules unless the rest of the hand already curves well
Absorbing Bloom without early characters capable of challenging
Main weakness

The deck is heavily dependent on maintaining characters and suitable Shift targets. Early removal aimed at Nick, Judy and Darkwing can leave expensive cards stranded in your hand. Preserve your Shift bases when possible, and do not ink all copies of your smaller named characters just because you already have one in play.
cards: 4 The Terror That Flaps in the Night
4 Darkwing Duck & Launchpad - St. Canard's Finest
